## Open PRs

proposals.title: Link new feature docs from README
proposals.detail: Ensure docs/dream.md, docs/factory.md, docs/qa.md, docs/readiness.md, docs/review.md, docs/wiki.md, and docs/automations.md are linked from README.md so humans and agents discover the full surface without reading the crate tree.

proposals.title: Persist consolidated memory from nightly dream
proposals.detail: Seed Project Memory (Stack/Commands/Gotchas/Conventions) from this dream output and keep Lessons/Failures updated after each mission so future agents do not start from empty memory.

proposals.title: Smoke tests for readiness_v2 and dream/qa modules
proposals.detail: Add a minimal unit/integration test around readiness_v2 reporting (especially the code-review check) and a smoke test that dream/qa modules load without panicking, reusing existing tests/ and QA skill rails.

proposals.title: Install code-review automation to hit 100% readiness
proposals.detail: Run `aegis install-code-review` (or wire crates/aegis-core/src/review.rs + docs/review.md into CI) to clear the sole failed readiness check and reach full L5 Product & Experimentation coverage.

proposals.title: Commit untracked dream/factory/qa/wiki surface
proposals.detail: Stage and commit the untracked dream/factory/qa/readiness/review/wiki/automations modules, docs, .aegis/, aegis-qa workflow, and dream-cycle asset with a clear CHANGELOG entry so nightly dream and factory rails are reproducible on a clean clone.

reflection_summary: Aegis is a Rust workspace agent (CLI + core) at L5 readiness 95%, blocked only on code-review automation, with rich but still-untracked dream/factory/qa/wiki/review surface and empty project memory. This dream consolidates stack/commands/gotchas/conventions, records durable lessons about readiness and commit hygiene, and proposes installing review automation, committing the new modules/docs, persisting memory, and adding light smoke tests—favoring memory and small rails over refactors.
